The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

In Theaters Fandango at Home

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

What to Know

Critics Consensus

Packed with colorful world-building that's as frenetic as it is weightless, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie's visuals are often out of this world but the threadbare story ultimately loses its Milky Way.

Read Critics Reviews
Audience Says

Mamma Mia! Nintendo nostalgia hits again in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, delivering Easter eggs, joyful wonder, and a spirited cast that make this an instant replay.

Read Audience Reviews

Critics Reviews

View More
Zaki Hasan San Francisco Chronicle 4d
Given the first film's success and the fanbase it helped build for this sequel, you can't help but wonder why the instinct is still to go wider instead of deeper, louder instead of more heartfelt. Go to Full Review Wendy Ide Observer (UK) 6d
The problem is not that it’s messy﻿, but ﻿that it’s stultifyingly dull. Go to Full Review Sara Michelle Fetters MovieFreak.com Apr 5
1/4
I found it all deeply annoying. Go to Full Review David Griffiths Subculture Entertainment 20h
3/5
This was never going to be a deeply serious film or even a game changer in the genre. But I can guarantee that if you are a Super Mario lover or are simply looking for a fun film for the whole family then you will not be disappointed. Go to Full Review Sarah Vincent Sarah G Vincent Views 1d
As someone who finds pure adventure and many video game movies boring, "The Super Mario Galaxy Movie" is a pure popcorn movie that adults will want to see even if they do not have kids to entertain. Go to Full Review Jon Winkler InBetweenDrafts 2d
5/10
It’s sad to call a movie booming with decades of imaginative characters and settings "disposable," but there aren’t enough Pikmin in the world to distract you from the blatant franchise baiting here. Go to Full Review Read all reviews

Audience Reviews

View More
Zander 3h Fantastic movie and highly recommend! It was honestly better than the first movie and the cameos are a nice surprise :) See more Katie 4h This brought out the kid in me and I was flooded with fond memories playing Super Mario Bros. with my brother on his old school Nintendo. Amazingly colorful, with non-stop action and loads of fun, I loved it. See more Nisha 4h It was cute and enjoyable just felt a bit rushed See more Nitra 5h Love all the new characters! Constant action. See more WillyWill 6h More action and interesting story line plus intro to new characters , this movie is for families and gamers , after playing Nintendo for yrs and version of games , now actually seeing those characters in the big screen is a dream come true . See more bob 6h Felt way to rushed but still incredibly enjoyable See more Read all reviews

Watchlist Tomatometer Popcornmeter
94% Tomatometer 385 Reviews 96% Popcornmeter 10,000+ Verified Ratings
Science teacher Ryland Grace (Ryan Gosling) wakes up on a spaceship light years from home with no recollection of who he is or how he got there. As his memory returns, he begins to uncover his mission: solve the riddle of the mysterious substance causing the sun to die out. He must call on his scientific knowledge and unorthodox ideas to save everything on Earth from extinction... but an unexpected friendship means he may not have to do it alone.
Where to Watch What to Know Reviews Cast & Crew Movie Clips More Like This Related News Videos Photos Media Info

Where to Watch

Project Hail Mary

In Theaters Fandango at Home

Rent Project Hail Mary on Fandango at Home, or buy it on Fandango at Home.

Project Hail Mary

What to Know

Critics Consensus

A visually dazzling space odyssey that's carried along effortlessly by the gravitational pull of Ryan Gosling at his most winning, Project Hail Mary is a near-miraculous fusion of smarts and heart.

Read Critics Reviews
Audience Says

One stellar Gosling + one arachnid-ish alien + one Hail Mary mission = lightning in a spaceship.

Read Audience Reviews

Critics Reviews

View More
Keith Uhlich (All (Parentheses)) (Substack) Apr 6
Among the many things I didn’t need in my life was a stealth sequel to Half Nelson…in space. Go to Full Review David Sims The Atlantic Apr 2
This film is not quite the masterpiece its grand visual scale sometimes suggests it’s shooting for. Still, it’s pretty consistently enjoyable, with a message that the viewing public could afford to hear. Go to Full Review Stephanie Zacharek TIME Magazine Mar 27
This is the most ambitious picture, in terms of structure and spectacle, that the filmmaking duo of Lord and Miller have ever made, and sometimes it reels out of their grasp. Go to Full Review Dorothy Woodend The Tyee (British Columbia) 14h
Adapted from Andy Weir’s 2021 novel, the film stays true to the major plot points of Weir’s work, with the bonus addition of Gosling’s incontrovertible charm to add to the action. Go to Full Review Michael Calleri Niagara Gazette 1d
Gosling is the audience’s representative on the screen and they’ll follow him anywhere, not because they have to, but because the actor has an everyman persona to which moviegoers will gravitate. His character is smart and likable. Go to Full Review Brooke Obie Black Girl Watching 2d
It’s unnecessarily long. And after decades of this patriarchal, white supremacist, American military propaganda, I’ve simply had enough. Go to Full Review Read all reviews

Audience Reviews

View More
Pluto 3h I have no words to describe how well and put together this was. See more Peter 4h Super movie, however too long. See more Christopher 5h Too many assumptions made to hurry the plot along awkwardly. The interaction with the space alien was implausible and childish. See more CHRISTOPHER R 5h Not even close to the book. It follows the basic plot, but the characters and action are completely different. It will disappoint you if you come from the book. See more RM 6h The movie makes more sense if you read the book first, but they did a great job bringing the book to life. See more Paul S 6h Disappointed. Hokie. Although a fantasy movie, some parts were not remotely believable and very far-fetched even within the story. See more Read all reviews

Watchlist Tomatometer Popcornmeter
94% Tomatometer 218 Reviews 94% Popcornmeter 5,000+ Verified Ratings
What if you could talk to animals and understand what they're saying? In Disney and Pixar's all-new feature film "Hoppers," scientists have discovered how to "hop" human consciousness into lifelike robotic animals, allowing people to communicate with animals as animals! What to Know

Critics Consensus

Flirting with complex themes, The Drama walks a tonal tightrope with impressive poise thanks to career-highlight performances by Robert Pattinson and Zendaya.

Read Critics Reviews
Audience Says

Elevated by remarkable performances, The Drama wrestles with questions of trust and forgiveness, drawing out profoundly uncomfortable emotions that linger long after the credits roll.

Read Audience Reviews

Critics Reviews

View More
Scott Tobias The Reveal 14h
3.5/5
A compelling high-wire act, wonky at times yet committed to living in discomfort. Go to Full Review David Sims The Atlantic 5d
In The Drama, Pattinson embodies Charlie as the model of tame, harmless Brit charm, and the minute the actor has built his character up, he clearly takes perverse delight in unraveling him. Go to Full Review Tre'vell Anderson Pop Culture Happy Hour (NPR Podcast) 6d
To see how they play with the comedy and the dark element of the comedy, I really liked it. It kept me interested in what was coming next. Go to Full Review Fico Cangiano CineXpress 13h
4/5
Unhinged, provocative, messy and funny, The Drama definitely delivers on, well... the drama. Includes banger performances by Zendaya and Pattison, great editing and music. And the last 20 minutes or so are a dandy. [Full review in Spanish] Go to Full Review Andrea Thompson A Reel of One's Own 16h
C-

4.5/5

A man trapped in a endless sterile subway passageway sets out to find Exit 8. The rules of his quest are simple: do not overlook anything out of the ordinary. If you discover an anomaly, turn back immediately. If you don’t, carry on. Then leave from Exit 8. But even a single oversight will send him back to the beginning. Will he ever reach his goal and escape this infinite corridor?
Where to Watch What to Know Reviews Cast & Crew Movie Clips Related News Videos Photos Media Info

Where to Watch

Exit 8

In Theaters Fandango at Home

Rent Exit 8 on Fandango at Home, or buy it on Fandango at Home.

Exit 8

What to Know

Critics Consensus

An unsettling maze navigated with finesse by director Genki Kawamura, Exit 8 is a video game adaptation rendered with existential dread and stylistic sophistication.

Read Critics Reviews

Critics Reviews

View More
Robert Abele Los Angeles Times 11h
With its bed made just so, “Exit 8” has a refreshing ambition: It wants to be both a purgatory and an advancement in form. When it smoothly moves between the two, it feels like a singular commentary on life’s deceptive banalities. Go to Full Review G. Allen Johnson San Francisco Chronicle 11h
2/4
It works for a while, but there’s only so much one can do with such a confining, empty storyline. Even at 95 minutes, “Exit 8” seems stretched out. There’s simply not enough there for a feature-length movie. Go to Full Review Brian Tallerico RogerEbert.com 3d
2.5/4
Still, there’s just enough for fans of the game or modern horror to chew on. If you wait to see it until PVOD or streaming, try watching it on a subway train on your way to work for maximum impact. Go to Full Review Abbie Bernstein Assignment X 11h
B
EXIT 8 takes getting lost in an urban maze and cranks the experience up to eleven. It’s existentially unsettling and occasionally genuinely scary. Go to Full Review Brogan Luke Bouwhuis InBetweenDrafts 12h
6/10
Kawamura delivering an entertaining film when the source material so deliberately rejected the norms of traditional storytelling is extremely impressive. Go to Full Review Charles Mudede The Stranger (Seattle, WA) 13h
Exit 8 turns out to be entertaining despite its minimal construction. The director also has an excellent sense of timing. Go to Full Review Read all reviews

aimee 8h A man finds himself in a repeating subway corridor and must identify anomalies to progress, with a single mistake resetting the loop. A subway corridor becomes an endless nightmare loop in Exit 8 haunting and immersive... but it's the stylish presentation and the strong camerawork and direction, inspired visuals, solid performances, and layers of intrigue that kept it engaging.. Great performances too.. It's all about the finding the wrong things and making the right moves. And fighting the dread of no escape.. Twenty years after making their iconic turns as Miranda, Andy, Emily and Nigel--Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci return to the fashionable streets of New York City and the sleek offices of Runway Magazine in 20th Century Studios' "The Devil Wears Prada 2," the eagerly awaited sequel to the 2006 phenomenon that defined a generation.
Where to Watch What to Know Reviews Cast & Crew Movie Clips Related News Videos Photos Media Info

Where to Watch

The Devil Wears Prada 2

In Theaters

The Devil Wears Prada 2

What to Know

Critics Consensus

Meryl Streep still wears Miranda Priestly like a finely-tailored suit in this sinfully enjoyable sequel, which is dressed to the nines in off-the-rack wish fulfillment and some trenchant observations about the state of modern media.

Read Critics Reviews
Audience Says

With its all-star cast dressed to the nines and an empowering story that fittingly expands the saga, The Devil Wears Prada 2 proves that a classic can be restyled with the right accessories -- and this one is a showstopper.

Read Audience Reviews

Critics Reviews

View More
Thelma Adams AARP Movies for Grownups 10h
4/5
Streep, Tucci, Hathaway and Blunt all step up to the dramedic challenge, even if the idea of “let’s all get along” at the end seems a bit forced. Go to Full Review Linda Holmes Pop Culture Happy Hour (NPR Podcast) 1d
I enjoyed seeing these people play these characters again. I enjoyed seeing them with each other again. But I think the story is extraordinarily thin. Go to Full Review Udita Jhunjhunwala Livemint 1d
The fashion is not as dramatic this time, and the dialogues not as meme-worthy, but the cast is on-point and the cameos are many. The result is a glossy, fun watch that’s also a commentary on an industry struggling in the digital age. Go to Full Review Sarah Marrs Lainey Gossip 11h
There is thematic heft under the wild style, and the comedy is grounded in a shared human experience of job uncertainty and trying to hold the line when it feels like someone higher up has already decided everyone’s fates. Go to Full Review Victoria Montalti Refinery29 12h
The catty and competitive natures of the fashion and media industries doesn’t go untouched, with plenty of drama to take us from start to finish. Go to Full Review Margaret Darby Deseret News (Salt Lake City) 12h
Although “The Devil Wears Prada 2″ scales the toxic, psychological roller coaster intensity that defines the original, it’s loaded with style, nostalgia, a new conflict and impressive performances that make for a worthwhile sequel. Go to Full Review Read all reviews

Critics Consensus
Anne Hathaway acquits herself well as a believable pop star in Mother Mary, a modish psychodrama that can be frustratingly obtuse but has style to spare.

Audience Says
Mother Mary may not be for the masses, but this intriguing film will captivate those seeking a bold, immersive, and trippy experience that's elevated by its stars.
